name: Tabasco Classic- Eula Mae's Cajun Seafood Gu
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Fresh Okra (or Two 16 oz Packages Frozen Okra, Thawed and Thinly Sliced)
1 Head Garlic, Cloves Peeled but Left Whole
16 oz Whole Tomatoes, Drained and Chopped
4 lb Medium Shrimp, Shelled and Deveined
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 1/2 cup of the oil in a large skillet (not cast iron) over medium heat. Add the okra and cook, stirring frequently, until it is no longer ropy, about 30 minutes.
2. Add the vinegar and cook, stirring, for another 10 minutes, until the okra takes on a brownish color and is reduced to about a quarter of its original volume. Set aside in a medium bowl.
3. In a large stockpot over high heat, bring the water to a boil. Meanwhile, add the remaining 1/4 cup oil and the ham to the skillet.
4. Saute the ham over medium-high heat for about 10 minutes, or until it is lightly browned.
5. With a slotted spoon, remove the ham to the stockpot.
6. In the same skillet, combine the onions, celery, garlic, and pepper; cook, stirring constantly, for 10 minutes, until the vegetables are tender.
7. Add the vegetables, okra, and tomatoes to the stockpot; cover and simmer over medium heat for 1 hour.
8. Reduce the heat to very low, add the shrimp, and simmer slowly for 10 minutes. Add the crabmeat and Tabasco sauce; simmer for an additional 5 to 10 minutes.
9. Serve the gumbo in soup bowls with scoops of rice.
